commit.message:
Feat/billing ba compliance gaps (#209)

* feat(billing): implement BA compliance gaps — 9 new commands + invoice finalization fix

Implements all high-priority gaps from pms_accounting_ba_v2 gap analysis.

Schema layer (schema/src/events/commands/billing.ts):
- BillingPaymentCaptureCommandSchema: +idempotency_key, +transaction_type enum
  (ADVANCE_DEPOSIT), +exchange_rate, +original_currency (G-08, G-09, G-13)
- BillingChargebackRecordCommandSchema: +initial_status enum, +evidence array (G-05)
- BillingFiscalPeriodCloseCommandSchema: +reconciliation_confirmed (G-15)
- 9 new command schemas: invoice.reopen, folio.reopen, folio.merge,
  chargeback.update_status, no_show.charge, late_checkout.charge,
  tax_exemption.apply, cancellation.penalty, comp.post

Handler layer (billing-service):
- G-01: Invoice number now assigned at FINALIZE via nextval() inside FOR UPDATE
  lock — eliminates sequence gaps from abandoned drafts
- G-02: reopenInvoice — marks original SUPERSEDED, creates DRAFT at revision+1
- G-03: reopenFolio — FOR UPDATE lock, validates SETTLED/CLOSED, sets OPEN
- G-04: mergeFolios — deadlock-safe UUID-ordered dual lock, re-attributes
  postings, closes source folio
- G-05: updateChargebackStatus state machine (RECEIVED→EVIDENCE_SUBMITTED→WON|LOST)
  + auto reopenFolio on LOST outcome
- G-10: chargeNoShow — validates reservation status, posts DEBIT, marks NO_SHOW
- G-14: applyTaxExemption — sets tax_exempt=true, records certificate on folio
- G-16: chargeCancellationPenalty — rate plan or override amount, CANCEL_PENALTY
- G-18: chargeLateCheckout — tier logic (<=2h = 50%, >2h = 100% of room_rate)
- G-24: postComp — CREDIT posting + comp_transactions budget record,
  ROOM type requires authorized_by

Gateway (api-gateway):
- 9 new POST command routes for all new operations
- tenantReservationParamsSchema added to import for reservation routes

Wiring:
- command-center-consumer.ts: 9 new switch cases
- billing-commands/index.ts: exports all new functions
- billing-commands/schemas.ts: re-exports all 9 new schemas

DB seed:
- 9 new command_templates rows in 10_command_center.sql

HTTP tests:
- billing.http: 11 new test blocks covering all new commands

Closes BA gaps: G-01, G-02, G-03, G-04, G-05 (partial), G-08, G-09,
G-10, G-13, G-14, G-15, G-16, G-18, G-24

* feat: add commission management, fiscal periods, and invoice management components

- Implemented CommissionsComponent for managing commission reports with date filtering and sorting.
- Created FiscalPeriodsComponent to manage accounting periods with options to close, lock, and reopen periods.
- Developed InvoicesComponent for creating, adjusting, and finalizing guest invoices, including status filtering and pagination.
- Added corresponding HTML and SCSS files for each component to define structure and styles.

* feat: add dependabot configuration for npm and GitHub Actions

- Introduced a new `.github/dependabot.yml` file to manage dependency updates.
- Configured weekly updates for npm packages and GitHub Actions with specific grouping for related dependencies.

docs: create Accounts & Billing implementation roadmap

- Added `Accounts.TODO.md` detailing the implementation roadmap for accounts and billing features.
- Organized into phases with priority levels and specific implementation tasks for critical business logic, financial compliance, missing features, and advanced features.
- Included references to BA requirements and existing code for context.

* Refactor code structure for improved readability and maintainability

* feat(gaps): Implement various financial management enhancements

- GAP-02: Introduce Advance Deposit Ledger to manage deposits as liabilities instead of revenue.
- GAP-03: Create Suspense Account for unroutable charges to prevent revenue leakage.
- GAP-04: Add Payment Gateway Webhook Handling for asynchronous payment status updates.
- GAP-05: Establish POS/HTNG Integration Endpoint for seamless charge posting.
- GAP-06: Implement GL/ERP Batch Export after Night Audit for financial reporting.
- GAP-07: Integrate Loyalty Program for points accrual and redemption management.
- GAP-08: Develop Financial Approval Workflows to enforce dual authorization on high-risk operations.
- GAP-09: Write financial audit trail records for compliance and forensics.
- GAP-10: Handle overpayments with configurable options for refunds or credits.
- GAP-11: Ensure invoice and credit note sequential numbering for compliance.
- GAP-12: Snapshot cancellation policies at booking time to avoid disputes.
- GAP-13: Lock FX rates for multi-currency transactions to ensure accurate billing.
- GAP-14: Ensure atomicity and rollback capabilities for night audit processes.
- GAP-15: Implement locking mechanisms for concurrent payment and checkout operations.
- GAP-16: Map charge codes to USALI GL accounts for accurate financial reporting.
- GAP-17: Automate Group Master Billing management for group bookings.
- GAP-18: Create UI for Chargeback Management to streamline dispute handling.
- GAP-19: Develop UI for Credit Note Management to facilitate credit issuance.
- GAP-20: Enhance Folio Detail View with actions for better financial management.
- GAP-21: Implement UI for Folio Routing Rules Management to streamline charge routing.
- GAP-22: Create UI for Deposit Schedule Management to track deposit payments.
- GAP-23: Develop UI for Financial Audit Log Viewer for compliance tracking.
- GAP-24: Implement UI for General Ledger Batch Viewer for financial oversight.

* Update Apps/billing-service/src/services/billing-commands/comp-post.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address all PR #209 review comments

- no-show-charge: use CONFIRMED status (RESERVED not in reservation_status enum)
- comp-post: map comp_type to comp_category DB enum values (FOOD_BEVERAGE→FOOD,
  ACTIVITY/MISCELLANEOUS→OTHER); add guest_id to comp_transactions INSERT (NOT NULL);
  fetch guest_id from folio; fix docstring (no budget enforcement)
- folio.ts: fix mergeFolios to absorb all source totals (charges/credits/payments)
  separately into target; zero total_payments on source close to satisfy balance
  check constraint; fix non-null assertion warnings
- chargeback.ts: inline folio reopen inside updateChargebackStatus transaction
  to avoid nested withTransaction deadlock; remove incorrect property_id placeholder;
  remove now-unused reopenFolio import
- fiscal-period: reject close when reconciliation_confirmed === false
- billing-routes: fix chargeback status route param (paymentId→refundId);
  add tenantRefundParamsSchema; route now uses /chargebacks/:refundId/status
- core-service tenants.ts/system-tenants.ts: remove tenant_id from users INSERT
  (column does not exist on users table); set mfa_secret to NULL instead of
  hardcoded dummy value
- night-audit UI: reset deptTotalGross/deptTotalNet/totalTaxCollected to 0 on
  load error to prevent stale values

* fix(tests): correct DB mock param indices for INSERT INTO tenants and users

The mock was reading params[0] as name and params[1] as slug for INSERT INTO
tenants, but all tenant queries pass the UUID as params[0], making name=params[1]
and slug=params[2]. Similarly, the users mock assumed params[0]=username but
system-tenants.ts and tenants.ts include id as the first parameter.

- Fix INSERT INTO tenants mock: use params[1]=name, params[2]=slug
- Fix INSERT INTO users mock: detect (id, ...) prefix via sql content;
  offset lookups by 1 when id is the first column

Fixes 2 failing tests in system-admin.test.ts:
- 'allows system admins to create tenants' (slug mismatch)
- 'allows system admins to bootstrap tenants' (400 from Zod email validation
  failure because email ended up as a UUID string)

commit.message:
fix(time): parse date-only strings as UTC midnight to prevent day-1 offset

Date-only inputs (YYYY-MM-DD) were parsed as Asia/Shanghai midnight,
which when stored in @db.Date columns (UTC) would shift back one day.
Now parsed as UTC midnight, preserving the intended calendar date.

Affects: Semester.startDate/endDate, Exam.examDate, Schedule.date

commit.message:
ci: fix release and performance workflows

- Dockerfile: Install all deps (including devDeps) for TypeScript build, then prune for production
- release.yml: Make Docker job continue-on-error, decouple notify from Docker success
- performance.yml: Create benchmark/load-test scripts inside packages/mcp-server so require() resolves correctly (was creating at repo root but running from package dir)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Bump TypeSpec emitter versions to latest (#38075)

### Packages impacted by this PR

`eng/emitter-package.json`, `eng/emitter-package-lock.json`,
`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`

### Issues associated with this PR

Bump emitter version

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

TypeSpec emitter dependencies in `eng/emitter-package.json` were pinned
to stale versions. Updated all packages to latest:

| Package | Old | New |
|---------|-----|-----|
| `@azure-tools/typespec-ts` | `0.52.0` | `0.52.1` |
| `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core` | `0.66.1` | `0.67.0` |
| `@azure-tools/typespec-autorest` | `0.66.1` | `0.67.0` |
| `@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core` | `0.66.4` | `0.67.1` |
| `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager` | `0.66.1` | `0.67.0` |
| `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-rulesets` | `0.66.0` | `0.67.0` |
| `@azure-tools/openai-typespec` | `1.13.1` | `1.14.0` |
| `@typespec/compiler` | `1.10.0` | `1.11.0` |
| `@typespec/http` | `1.10.0` | `1.11.0` |
| `@typespec/openapi` | `1.10.0` | `1.11.0` |
| `@typespec/openapi3` | `1.10.0` | `1.11.0` |
| `@typespec/rest` | `0.80.0` | `0.81.0` |
| `@typespec/versioning` | `0.80.0` | `0.81.0` |
| `@typespec/events` | `0.80.0` | `0.81.0` |
| `@typespec/streams` | `0.80.0` | `0.81.0` |

Also bumped `@autorest/typescript` range in
`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`: `^6.0.66` → `^6.0.67`.

Lock file regenerated via `tsp-client generate-lock-file`.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

N/A — straightforward version bump.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — dependency version updates only; no logic changes.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

N/A

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release
request PRs)_

```bash
tsp-client generate-lock-file
```

### Checklists
- [ ] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here)_

commit.message:
Fix devtunnel connection exhaustion: persistent HTTP client + slow poll

The remote fetcher loop was creating new TCP connections every 5 seconds,
overwhelming the devtunnel relay and causing it to drop within seconds.

Three fixes:

1. Remote poll interval: 5s → 30s with exponential backoff on failure
   (up to 5 minutes). Sequential node polling instead of parallel burst.

2. Persistent HTTP client: MCPTransport now reuses a single
   httpx.AsyncClient instead of creating/closing one per request.
   This reuses TCP connections through the relay tunnel.

3. Connection recovery: stale clients are closed and recreated on
   errors instead of accumulating broken connections.

Before: ~12 new TCP connections per minute through relay → tunnel dies
After:  ~2 requests per minute reusing 1 TCP connection → stable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove all ./.dotnet/dotnet fallback instructions after prerequisites
- Simplify workload section to use plain dotnet commands
- Replace duplicate 'Undo all changes' section with link to Clean up

* Workload section: clarify running from global.json folder, verify with dotnet --info

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Workload commands: document DOTNET_ROOT gap, use local binary

Workload commands (install, list) store metadata relative to the dotnet
root of the host process. When using the system host with sdk.paths,
workloads end up in the system installation rather than the local one.
This is a known gap (dotnet/sdk#49825).

Updated all workload examples to use ./.dotnet/dotnet directly, with
an IMPORTANT callout explaining why and linking to the tracking issue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
